title: The Chain Master
subtitle: True power comes from combining simple commands

briefing.narrative:
The monk nods approvingly at your progress. "You're almost ready for the mountain. But there's one more art you must learn — the art of chaining."

He pulls out a thick scroll. "The monastery library has a catalog of ancient texts. Your task: find the entry about 'meditation' and write it to a new file. You'll need grep to search and the ability to chain commands together."

He winks. "The monks up at the monastery chain commands like breathing. You'll thank me later."
briefing.concept: 'grep' searches for text inside files. 'grep word file.txt' shows lines containing that word. You can chain commands with '&&' — the second command runs only if the first succeeds. For example: 'echo "hello" > file.txt && cat file.txt' creates a file then displays it.
objectives: Use 'grep' to find the line about meditation in catalog.txt
Do it in one line using && to chain commands

fileTree.catalog.txt:
history - The Founding of the Monastery (shelf A3)
cooking - 101 Recipes for Monastery Gruel (shelf B1)
meditation - The Art of Inner Peace (shelf C7)
gardens - Growing Herbs in Silence (shelf D2)
poetry - Haikus About Merge Conflicts (shelf E5)
cats - Why the Monastery Cat Rules Everything (shelf F1)

hints.text: The monk hints: 'grep meditation catalog.txt will find the line you need.'
He draws in the sand: 'echo "meditation" > found.txt creates the file. Or get fancy: grep meditation catalog.txt > found.txt redirects grep output to a file!'
The monastery cat types with practiced ease: grep meditation catalog.txt > found.txt

tips: 'grep word file' searches for lines containing 'word'
You can redirect output to a file: grep word file.txt > result.txt
